data.title: NetEase Shares Jump After Better-Than-Expected Results
data.description:
By Sherry Qin

Chinese videogame giant NetEase's shares surged in Hong Kong after it reported better-than-expected first-quarter results, suppored by the continued recovery in its gaming business.

NetEase's shares jumped 13.5% to 190.80 Hong Kong dollars Friday, on track for their largest daily percentage gain in over three years. Its American Depositary Shares advanced 15% overnight.

The Hangzhou-based company said Thursday that its net profit rose 35% to 10.30 billion yuan, equivalent to $1.43 billion, in the first three months of the year. That topped the estimate from a FactSet-compiled poll and marked a second consecutive quarter of increased profits.

Its online-gaming revenue grew 15% to 23.4 billion yuan, as both the sustained popularity of legacy flagship titles like "Identity V" and the recently launched ones like "Marvel Rivals" supported the sector.

Besides higher revenue, online game services' gross margin came in at 68.8%, beating market expectations. Analysts think the strength of high-margin legacy titles and new self-developed ones likely offset the margin dilution from Blizzard-licensed titles.

NetEase's cost discipline was another highlight of the latest quarter's report, with operating expenses down 14% sequentially. Nomura analysts noted that NetEase has revamped its marketing strategy to concentrate its capital resources on the most promising products.

The strong results prompted some banks to raise their target prices for NetEase's shares.

Citi raised NetEase's ADR target price to US$156.00 from US$118.00 while Barclays adjusted it to US$118.00 from US$104.00.

NetEase has been searching for its next blockbuster title. The recent successes of newly launched "Marvel Rivals" and "Where Winds Meet," could stoke hope for the videogame maker.

"The initial successes of these new game releases have set a positive tone for 2025, but we believe it is still too early to conclude they will remain popular." Barclays analysts said in a research note.

Write to Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com

data.title: Kyoto Financial Group's Net Income Rises 16% in Fiscal 2024; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
Kyoto Financial Group's net income attributable to owners of the parent rose 16% to 36.55 billion yen for fiscal 2024 from 31.57 billion yen a year ago.

The company's net income per share rose to 125.03 yen from 106.47 yen a year ago, according to a Tokyo bourse filing on May 15.

Ordinary income jumped 21.4% to 167.26 billion yen from 137.69 billion yen a year earlier.

The company declared a year-end dividend of 30 yen per share for fiscal 2024, payable from June 3.

For fiscal 2025, the company expects attributable net income to grow 9.4% to 40.00 billion yen, or 137.94 yen per share, while ordinary income is projected to increase 19.3% to 199.70 billion yen.

The company plans to pay interim and year-end dividends of 35 yen per share, each, for the current year.

Shares of the company declined over 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Samyang Foods' Shares Surge, Led by Strong Quarterly Results
data.description:
By Kwanwoo Jun

Samyang Foods' shares surged Friday, led by strong first-quarter results and upbeat market expectations for even higher revenue and earnings in the following quarters.

The South Korean manufacturer of instant noodles and other food products said after Thursday's market close that revenue and operating profit jumped 37% and 67%, respectively, from a year earlier, beating street views and hitting quarterly records for the January-March period.

The stock rose as much as 24% in early Friday trading, staying on course to post its sharpest daily gain in one year and outperforming the benchmark Kospi's 0.1% gain.

Market analysts say brisk demand for Samyang's flagship spicy, chicken-flavored instant-noodle brand, Buldak, and other snacks boosted the company's results, aided by the recent spread of Korean pop culture globally.

Hanwha Investment & Securities analyst Han Yu-jung said in a research note Friday that strong revenue growth from overseas markets drove Samyang's above-consensus first-quarter results, with sales surging 77% from a year earlier in the U.S. and 31% from a year earlier in China.

Han said he expects Samyang to post new quarterly records for revenue and operating profit in the April-June period, which is set to reflect the previous quarter's delayed shipments to the U.S. as well as increased production on brisk demand.

IBK Securities analyst Kim Tae-hyun said Friday he expects Samyang's instant-noodle production capacity to grow 40%. Samyang is conducting test runs at new production lines in South Korea that are scheduled to begin commercial operations in July. Kim said he expects the new lines to boost Samyang's overall revenue by more than 60%.

Hanwha Investment and IBK raised their target prices for Samyang by 42% and 34%, respectively, and maintained their buy rating on the stock.

Write to Kwanwoo Jun at kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com

data.title: New Zealand Q2 Inflation Expectations Highest in A Year
data.description:
The Reserve Bank of New Zealand’s (RBNZ) quarterly survey of expectations revealed that business managers now see inflation averaging 2.29% over the next two years as of Q2 2025, up from 2.06% in the previous quarter—the highest in a year.

One-year inflation expectations also rose to 2.41% from 2.15%r—the highest in four quarters.

Longer-term projections showed five-year-ahead inflation expectations edging up to 2.18% from 2.13%, while 10-year expectations increased to 2.15% from 2.07%.

As for interest rates, respondents forecast the Official Cash Rate (OCR) to stand at 3.77% by the end of Q1 2025, before declining to 3.23% by year-end.

data.title: Houston-Based Agency Jeremy McGilvrey Launches AI-Driven Conversion Optimization Service for Businesses
data.description:
Houston, Texas--(Newsfile Corp. - May 15, 2025) - The Houston-based digital marketing and web design agency Jeremy McGilvrey has announced the launch of an AI-powered conversion optimization service. The new offering uses machine learning and behavioral analytics to help businesses improve website and funnel performance through data-informed decision-making and real-time user behavior tracking.

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/8843/252280_jeremy_mcgilvrey.jpg

Developed over several years of applied work in web strategy and UX optimization, the service is built to help companies identify user drop-off points, bottlenecks, and missed engagement opportunities across their digital ecosystems. Unlike traditional split testing or standard analytics platforms, the system continuously monitors user behavior and provides adaptive performance recommendations as data evolves.

"Most businesses don't have a traffic problem, they have a conversion problem," said Jeremy McGilvrey, founder and CEO of the agency. "This new service goes far beyond standard testing. It gives clients a dynamic system that learns, adapts, and improves, without relying on guesswork."

Applied Behavioral Data Meets Automated Decision Support

The conversion optimization service leverages AI and predictive modeling to analyze how users move through websites and funnels. With real-time insights into engagement patterns, load times, and form drop-off points, clients are able to pinpoint exactly where user experience is breaking down, and how to fix it.

This approach enables updates to:

  • Page structure and visual hierarchy
  • Headline clarity and call-to-action performance
  • Checkout and form flow design
  • Device responsiveness and mobile optimization
  • Personalized user targeting based on live segmentation

The agency reports that clients using the service have seen improved on-site retention, increased lead quality, and measurable lifts in average order value, without expanding ad budgets.

Tailored for Growth-Focused Businesses

Jeremy McGilvrey's conversion optimization service is designed for companies generating traffic but experiencing underperformance in key conversion areas. The program is particularly suited to e-commerce brands, consultants, SaaS firms, and education platforms focused on scaling their digital performance.

Included in the service are:

  • Full-funnel performance audits and diagnostics
  • Clickstream heat mapping and behavior analysis
  • Machine learning-powered optimization roadmaps
  • Real-time A/B testing support
  • Monthly reporting tied to revenue outcomes

Clients may choose between advisory implementation or full-service execution delivered by the agency's internal optimization team.

Agency Expertise in Strategic, Conversion-Focused Design

Jeremy McGilvrey agency has worked with over 1,000 businesses worldwide across multiple verticals, combining high-level strategy with data-driven execution. The firm's reputation is built on user-first design, digital trust architecture, and a commitment to marketing systems that convert without gimmicks.

The launch of the AI-powered service is part of the agency's broader initiative to combine psychology, automation, and performance data into scalable infrastructure that helps businesses grow smarter, not louder.

About Jeremy McGilvrey (Agency)

Jeremy McGilvrey is a Houston-based digital marketing and web strategy agency specializing in high-conversion website design, sales funnel development, and behavioral optimization. The firm's work has been featured in Forbes, Entrepreneur, Business Insider, and NBC. Led by founder and CEO Jeremy McGilvrey, the agency supports clients through ethical, system-based solutions to long-term growth.

Media Contact

Jeremy McGilvrey

2800 Post Oak Blvd. Ste. 5600B

Houston, TX 77056

Phone: (832) 390-2229

Email: hi@jeremymcgilvrey.com

Website: https://www.jeremymcgilvrey.com

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/252280

data.title: Cathay Financial Logs NT$32 Billion Profit in Q1
data.description:
Cathay Financial reported a profit attributable to owners of parent of NT$32.0 billion, according to a Thursday filing with the Taiwan Stock Exchange.

Basic earnings per share came in at NT$2.18, while net revenue was NT$106.3 billion.

The insurer did not disclose year-ago figures.

data.title: Market Chatter: Bayer Explores Bankruptcy for Monsanto Unit Amid Attempts to Settle Roundup Cases
data.description:
Bayer is considering filing bankruptcy for its Monsanto agriculture business if a settlement with plaintiffs fails in class actions related to allegations that its Roundup weedkiller causes cancer, The Wall Street Journal reported Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The unnamed sources said the German drug and pesticide maker is making plans to resolve the class actions through proceedings in the US state court of Missouri, adding that the bankruptcy is a backup plan, according to the report.

The company sought the assistance of restructuring advisers from law firm Latham & Watkins and consultant AlixPartners as it considers the options, the people noted.

Bayer did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

data.title: Kaiser Reef Completes Acquisition of Catalyst Metals' Tasmania Gold Mine, Tenements
data.description:
Kaiser Reef completed the acquisition of the Henty gold mine and associated Tasmanian exploration tenements from Catalyst Metals , according to a Friday Australian Securities Exchange filing.

Catalyst now holds a 19.99% stake in Kaiser, and both firms are considering the potential joint development of the Maldon processing plant.

Kaiser Reef completed the second tranche of a AU$30 million placement through the issue of 149.1 million fully-paid ordinary shares at AU$0.14 per share.

It also secured a further AU$10 million of funding with Auramet International, per the filing. Per a facility agreement with Auramet, it also issued Auramet 3 million options, exercisable at AU$0.20 per option and expiring on May 16, 2027.

Kaiser Reef's shares fell 3% in recent Friday trade, while Catalyst Metals' shares surged 5%.

data.title: Copper prices set for weekly gain on US-China trade optimism
data.description:
Copper prices inched higher on Friday and were on track to end the week in positive territory, supported by a China-U.S. tariff truce, although concerns over longer-term demand for the metal capped further gains.

Asian stocks were poised for a strong week on a softer note as the euphoria over U.S.-China trade talks faded, while revived bets for policy easing in the United States sparked a rally in beaten-down bond markets.

Benchmark copper on the London Metal Exchange (LME) was up 0.1% at $9,585 a metric ton, as of 0238 GMT, and the most-traded copper contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) edged 0.1% higher to 78,430 yuan ($10,889.43) per ton.

"Chinese traders are happy about the 90-day pause, but the market has remained uncertain about what is going to happen after the 90 days," a Shanghai-based metals said. "The fact that China's exporters have been rushing cargoes out is a tell-tale sign," she added.

For the week, LME copper is up about 1.5%, while prices in Shanghai have risen around 1%.

However, the initial optimism has faded about a 90-day pause agreed by Beijing and Washington on most of their retaliatory tariffs. The market is focusing on the potential new tariffs on copper imports that the U.S. has been conducting since February too.

Among other London metals, aluminium edged 0.1% higher to $2,492 per ton, zinc dipped 0.1% to $2,723 a ton and lead lost 0.4% to $1,996.5 and Tin rose 0.1% to $33,000.

Analysts expect prices to hover around 78,000–79,000 yuan per ton in the near term, reflecting mixed market sentiment.

SHFE aluminium fell 0.3% to 20,185 yuan a ton, zinc dropped 0.6% to 22,595 yuan and lead slipped 0.5% to 16,920 yuan.

For the top stories in metals and other news, click

or

($1 = 7.2024 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Helia Says CBA, Affiliates Become Substantial Holder
data.description:
Helia Group received notice that Commonwealth Bank of Australia and its affiliates became substantial holders of the lender's mortgage insurance provider, effective Thursday, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The bank and its affiliates own 13.7 million shares in the company, representing a 5.03% total voting power, the filing said.

data.title: Hillgrove Resources Says Freepoint Metals Sells South Australian Project's Royalty to Vox Royalty; Shares Rise 6%
data.description:
Hillgrove Resources said the royalty of its South Australian Kanmantoo gold and copper project has been acquired from Freepoint Metals & Concentrates by Vox Royalty, a Toronto Stock Exchange and Nasdaq-listed mining royalty company, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The company's shares rose 6% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Market Chatter: Hanwha Hotels Acquires 58.62% Stake in Ready-to-Eat Meals Firm Ourhome for KRW869.5 Billion
data.description:
Hanwha subsidiary Hanwha Hotels & Resorts acquired a 58.62% stake in domestic ready-to-eat meal supplier Ourhome for 869.5 billion won, Yonhap News reported Thursday.

The deal, approved by regulators last month, involves shares purchased from Koo family members Koo Bon-seong, Koo Mi-hyun, and two related stakeholders, the report said.

data.title: Yuyue Medical Equipment & Supply Obtains Gains EU MDR Certification for External Defibrillator
data.description:
Jiangsu Yuyue Medical Equipment & Supply subsidiary Metrax GmbH received class three European Union Medical Devices Regulation certification for its HeartSave myPAD automated external defibrillator.

The device is used in suspected sudden cardiac arrest to guide the operator to start and perform resuscitation, analyze the patient's electrocardiogram and provide defibrillation treatment, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The certification is valid until May 11, 2030.

Shares of the medical equipment maker were down less than 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Air China's Passenger Traffic Rises 8.6% in April
data.description:
Air China's combined passenger traffic, which is measured by revenue passenger kilometers or RPK, rose 8.6% year on year to 23.7 billion in April, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

The Chinese airline's passenger capacity climbed 5.3% to 29.2 billion available seat kilometers or ASK from the prior year.

The company's cargo capacity during the same month inched 8.7% higher to 1.1 billion available freight tonne kilometers, while cargo and mail traffic rose 10.3% to 407.9 million revenue freight tonne kilometers.

Air China's Hong Kong shares rose more than 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Reject Shop Says Federal Court Orders Meeting for Proposed Acquisition by Canada's Dollarama
data.description:
Reject Shop said the Federal Court of Australia ordered that a meeting of its shareholders be held to decide on the company's proposed acquisition by Toronto Stock Exchange-listed Dollarama, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The scheme meeting is scheduled for June 23, the filing said.

The company's board recommends shareholders vote in favor of the scheme, subject to the absence of a superior proposal and an independent expert's conclusion that it is in the best interests of shareholders, the filing added.

data.title: Japan 10-Year Yield Holds Steady Despite GDP Miss
data.description:
Japan’s 10-year government bond yield held around 1.47% on Friday, near a six-week high, as investors weighed weak GDP data against expectations for further policy normalization.

The Japanese economy contracted 0.2% quarter-on-quarter in Q1, its first decline in a year and worse than the anticipated 0.1% drop.

Despite this, the Bank of Japan reiterated earlier this week that it expects upward pressure on wages and prices to persist, reinforcing its plans to continue gradually tightening monetary policy.

Investors are also watching progress in US-Japan trade negotiations, with Tokyo aiming to secure a deal by June.

Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba emphasized that any agreement must include provisions on automobiles and called on Washington to lift its 25% tariff on Japanese vehicles.

data.title: New Zealand Dollar Rebounds
data.description:
The New Zealand dollar rose to around $0.588 on Friday, ending a two-day losing streak as the US dollar fell on weak economic data that raised bets on more Federal Reserve rate cuts this year.

Latest data showed that US producer prices unexpectedly dropped by the most in five years, while retail sales growth slowed.

Domestically, markets are still wagering on a further cut of 25bps by the Reserve Bank of New Zealand later this month amid a weak economy.

However, investors suspect the central bank’s easing cycle may be nearing its end, with rates now seen bottoming at 3.0%.

Despite Friday’s rebound, the Kiwi is on track for its third consecutive weekly loss after a volatile week in which it saw gains on news of a US-China tariff deal, only to encounter selling pressure at key technical levels.

data.title: Japan's Economy Shrinks for the First Time in a Year — Update
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

TOKYO--Japan's economy shrank for the first time in a year and faces a bumpy road ahead due to the impact of U.S. trade policy.

Japan's real gross domestic product declined 0.2% in the January-March period from the previous quarter, preliminary government data showed Friday, putting the risk of a technical recession in play.

That compared with economists' forecast for a 0.1% contraction and the 0.6% growth recorded in the October-December quarter. The economy shrank 0.7% on an annualized basis in the first quarter.

The GDP data came amid concerns that U.S. tariffs could hurt Japan's hard-earned economic recovery by weighing on exports and making companies pull back on investment.

"With the economy already shrinking on the eve of the trade war, the Bank of Japan will probably wait even longer before resuming its tightening cycle than we had anticipated," said Marcel Thieliant, head of Asia-Pacific at Capital Economics.

Thieliant said it has become increasingly more difficult for the BOJ to raise interest rates in July as forecast.

Reflecting uncertainty about the global trade outlook, the BOJ recently halved its growth projection for the current fiscal year ending March 2026. While the central bank is still seeking further interest-rate increases, some economists say it may not be able to make any moves this year if trade frictions deepen.

In the first quarter, external demand, or exports minus imports, shaved 0.8 percentage point off growth, with exports down 0.6% and imports up 2.9%.

As U.S. tariffs hurt exports, one of Japan's main growth engines, persistent inflation has damaged another: private consumption, which accounts for about half of GDP. Friday's data showed that private spending was flat in the January-March period from the previous quarter.

"Continued price rises pose a downside risk to Japan's economy, affecting private consumption through a decline in consumer confidence," economy minister Ryosei Akazawa said at a news conference Friday.

Akazawa said tariff concerns haven't affected consumption yet, but the minister, who is in charge of trade negotiations with the U.S., promised to pay careful attention to consumers' mood after a recent government survey showed the possibility of worsening sentiment.

Solid corporate earnings have spurred companies to increase investments and offer pay raises. Capital expenditure was up 1.4% in the first three months of the year from the previous quarter.

Higher wages are expected to help the recovery in consumption, although food inflation has dimmed sentiment among households.

Credit Agricole economist Takuji Aida said the BOJ would need to confirm that the Japanese economy didn't deteriorate in the July-September period and update their forecasts in January before resuming rate hikes.

Some economists, including Aida, expect the Japanese economy to shrink again this quarter, pushing the nation into a technical recession, defined as two consecutive quarters of contraction.

There is a chance that the second estimate of first-quarter GDP will show an upward revision, moving the economy out of the recession danger zone. That said, an upgrade isn't likely to skew the outlook too positively, said Stefan Angrick, head of Japan and frontier markets economics at Moody's Analytics.

"Coming data won't change the picture of an economy that's struggling," he said in a note.

How negotiations between Tokyo and Washington proceed is another factor to consider.

Japanese officials have been asking the Trump administration to reconsider the levies, but whether a deal will be reached remains unclear. The two sides have agreed to hold another round of discussion in mid-May or later.

So far, "2025 is stacking up to be a difficult year as tariff pain and weak domestic momentum threaten to stifle growth," said Angrick.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: Cynata Therapeutics Says Potential Approval of CYP-001 Drug Candidate Unimpeded, Shares Jump 6%
data.description:
Cynata Therapeutics said the potential approval of its CYP-001 drug candidate will not be impeded by the orphan-drug exclusivity granted by the US Food and Drug Administration to Mesoblast's Ryoncil, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Ryoncil was granted the exclusivity period for the treatment of steroid-refractory acute graft versus host disease in paediatric patients aged two months of age and older.

CYP-001 is an off-the-shelf product for intravenous infusion based on a proprietary therapeutic stem cell platform technology. The FDA earlier granted orphan-drug designation to CYP-001 for the treatment of acute graft versus host disease, qualifying Cynata for incentives, including extended marketing exclusivity, tax credits, and fee waivers.

It emphasized that CYP-001 does not contain "the same drug" as Ryoncil, noting that the orphan-drug designation says that approval will not be given to a sponsor of the same drug for the same use or indication for seven years.

Its shares jumped 6% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Singapore Dollar Strengthens Amid Fed Rate-Cut Prospects — Market Talk
data.description: The Singapore dollar strengthens against its U.S. counterpart amid prospects of Fed rate cuts that would reduce the allure of U.S. fixed-income assets and demand for the greenback. Both U.S. retail sales and PPI data released overnight were softer than expected, MUFG Bank's Michael Wan says in a research report. This has resulted in some increased pricing for Fed rate cuts, with Treasury yields falling as a result, the senior currency analyst says. The 2-year U.S. Treasury yield is down 2bps at 3.9415% and the 10-year yield is down 3bps at 4.4212%, according to FactSet. USD/SGD is 0.2% lower at 1.2952. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: NetEase Hong Kong shares hit over 4-year peak after Q1 results beat
data.description:
** Shares of Chinese internet and game services provider NetEase Inc jump 16.1% to HK$195.2, their highest since February 2021, and on course for a third consecutive session of gains

** Stock on track for the biggest one-day pct rise since March 2022 if gains hold; top pct gainer on Hang Seng Index and Hang Seng Tech Index , which fall 1% and 0.9%, respectively

** U.S.-listed stock rose 14.6% on Thursday

** NetEase said its Q1 net revenues rose 7.4% y/y and net income rose 35.5% y/y

** Jefferies keeps "buy" rating and lifts PT of the U.S. shares to $131 from $120, after results and anticipating more new games

** Citi maintains "buy" and raises TP to $156 from $118, saying 1Q25 gaming revenue came stronger than expected

** YTD, Hong Kong shares up 38.4%, U.S. stock up 37.6%
